channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Canadian ministers head to Palm Beach for talks with incoming Trump administration
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Two top Canadian Cabinet ministers are visiting Palm Beach to talk border security and trade with the incoming Donald Trump administration as Trump threatens tariffs on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Richard Parsons, prominent Black executive who led Time Warner and Citigroup, dies at 76
item.description: Richard Parsons, one of corporate America’s most prominent Black executives who held top posts at Time Warner and Citigroup, has died.
item.dc:creator: Anick Jesdanun And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: India's former prime minister Manmohan Singh, architect of economic reforms, dies at 92
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program and a landmark nuclear deal with the United States, has died. He was 92.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Boy killed in Colorado reservation shooting was in bed when he was hit
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A 7-year-old boy who died in a shooting that left 24 bullet holes in a home on a tribal reservation in Colorado was lying next to his father in bed when he was hit, according to court documents unsealed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged Boston porch pirate dubbed the 'Tom Brady' of thefts arrested with bags of packages, police say
item.description: An alleged Massachusetts porch pirate being compared to NFL legend Tom Brady was arrested on Christmas Eve for multiple package thefts, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Peyote sacred to Native Americans threatened by psychedelic renaissance and development
item.description: HEBBRONVILLE, Texas (AP) — In this corner of southern Texas, the plump cacti seem to pop out of arid dust and cracked earth, like magic dumplings.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Career criminal, 31, arrested for 35th time
item.description: A Florida man and career criminal was arrested for his 35th time right before Christmas, after stealing a car and attempting to escape authorities in a high-speed chase.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drone sightings drop, but answers from feds remain limited
item.description: Federal and local investigators report a decrease in drone sightings while the FBI says there is nothing new to report
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burger King is giving away free chicken sandwiches and bacon cheeseburgers
item.description: Burger King is still spreading holiday cheer with after Christmas food deals. The burger chain has celebrated the holidays all month long with its 31-day deal promotion for rewards members and has saved some of its best deals for the end of the year. On Friday, Dec. 27, and Monday, Dec.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Powerful thunderstorms rumble across Texas, delaying holiday travel
item.description: Severe thunderstorms are firing up in parts of Texas and could trigger high winds, hail and potential tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida woman arrested after allegedly breaking in, stabbing person over pizza delivery tip
item.description: Armed with a knife, the suspect began attacking one of the victims, allegedly upset about the tip she received during an earlier delivery, deputies said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cat food distributed to 12 states recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Year-end travel: What you need to know about post-holiday trips
item.description: CHICAGO (NewsNation) — Millions of Americans are traveling on Thursday as a record-breaking post-holiday rush continues. The Transportation Security Administration anticipates Friday to be one of the season's busiest days, with 40 million passengers projected to be screened on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Feeling lucky? Jackpot for Mega Millions climbs to more than $1 billion
item.description: Hang onto those stocking-stuffer lottery tickets. The Mega Millions jackpot has surpassed $1 billion after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. That means the estimated jackpot for Friday's drawing is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The end of the 'Rust' criminal case against Alec Baldwin may unlock a civil lawsuit
item.description: The conclusion of a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in the fatal shooting of a cinematographer clears the way for a related civil lawsuit by relatives of the deceased woman and efforts to depose the actor under oath, attorneys for plaintiffs in the civil suit said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Heavy travel day off to a rough start after American Airlines briefly grounds all flights
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — American Airlines briefly grounded flights nationwide Tuesday because of a technical problem just as the Christmas travel season kicked into overdrive and winter weather threatened more potential problems for those planning to fly or drive.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Packers clinch playoff berth with 1st shutout in NFL this season, 34-0 over Saints
item.description: With a dominant defense leading the way, the Green Bay Packers clinched a playoff berth by producing their most lopsided win in a decade.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: FAYETTEVILLE, Ark. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing “harmful” materials to minors. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where is Santa? How to track his Christmas 2024 journey
item.description: Santa Claus, St. Nicholas, Babbo Natale, Père Noël — whatever you call him, the man clad in red will be here soon.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Nordstrom to be acquired by Nordstrom family and a Mexican retail group for $6.25B
item.description: ntury-old department store Nordstrom has agreed to be acquired and taken private by Nordstrom family members and a Mexican retail group for $6.25 billion, the company said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri company creates healthy dog treats using invasive fish
item.description: A Missouri company has found a way to turn an invasive fish into multiple useful products, including a new line of dog treats.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WGN 9

item.title: Louisiana often holds inmates past their release date, DOJ lawsuit claims
item.description: Louisiana’s prison system routinely holds people weeks and months after they have completed their sentences, the U.S. Department of Justice alleged in a lawsuit filed Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, ABC 4

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, WGN 9

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
